meaning: adj.被忽视的,未被观看的,未受监视的
examples: Things get stolen when it is easy to steal them because they are left lying around unwatched.
Across the restaurant was a frozen lake, fenceless, unwatched.
After lunch, I saw him move his croquet ball with hisfoot, thinking himself unwatched.
However, earlier in December Snapchat did issue a patch for a bug that put permanent versions of unwatched videos into the media gallery on Android phones.
